poojas.name: Ganapathi Homam
poojas.description: Sacred fire ritual to invoke Lord Ganesha's blessings for removing obstacles.

faq.question: When should Naamkaran be performed?
faq.answer: Naamkaran is typically performed on the 11th, 12th, or 14th day after birth. The exact date is determined based on the baby's birth chart and family traditions.

faq.question: What do we need to prepare?
faq.answer: You need to prepare a clean, sacred space for the ceremony. Our pandit will bring all necessary items including puja materials, flowers, and sacred texts.

faq.question: Can we choose the name?
faq.answer: Yes, you can suggest names, and the pandit will help select the most auspicious one based on the child's birth chart and astrological considerations.

poojas.name: Navagraha Homam
poojas.description: Fire ritual to appease nine planetary deities for cosmic balance and harmony.
poojas.fullDescription: Navagraha Homam is a comprehensive Vedic ritual dedicated to the nine celestial bodies that significantly influence human life. The nine planets (Grahas) are Surya (Sun), Chandra (Moon), Mangal (Mars), Budh (Mercury), Guru (Jupiter), Shukra (Venus), Shani (Saturn), Rahu (North Node), and Ketu (South Node). This sacred fire ceremony is performed to balance planetary influences, remove obstacles, and bring harmony to various aspects of life including career, health, relationships, and prosperity. Key Benefits: - Reduces malefic effects of unfavorable planetary positions - Enhances positive planetary influences - Brings balance to health and wellbeing - Improves career and financial prospects - Removes obstacles and delays in life - Promotes harmony in relationships - Provides spiritual growth and inner peace

faq.question: What is Navagraha Homam?
faq.answer: Navagraha Homam is a Vedic fire ritual performed to appease the nine planetary deities (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, and Ketu) to reduce their malefic effects and enhance their positive influences on one's life.

faq.question: When should this homam be performed?
faq.answer: It can be performed on auspicious days throughout the year. However, it's especially beneficial during times of planetary transitions, during Shukra Dasha, or when experiencing delays and obstacles in life.

faq.question: What items should we prepare at home?
faq.answer: You need to prepare a clean, well-ventilated space for the pooja. Our pandits will bring all necessary items including havan samagri, puja materials, and the sacred fire vessel. You may prepare fresh fruits and flowers if needed.

faq.question: How many people can participate?
faq.answer: Family members can actively participate in the ritual. Typically 10-15 people can comfortably participate. However, there's no strict limit - all family members are welcome to attend and benefit.

faq.question: What should I wear for the ceremony?
faq.answer: Wear clean, traditional Indian attire. For men: Dhoti/Kurta or white shirt with dhoti. For women: Saree or Salwar Kameez in light colors. Avoid black or red colored clothes.

faq.question: Can we book for a specific muhurta?
faq.answer: Yes, we can help you select an auspicious muhurta based on your birth chart. Please mention your requirement during booking, and our pandit will calculate the best time for you.

faq.question: Will we get prasadam and certificate?
faq.answer: Yes, prasadam (blessed food) will be distributed after the ceremony. Digital certificates are available upon request for premium packages.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: Our pandits can perform the ceremony in multiple languages including Hindi, Sanskrit, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, and English explanations. Please mention your preference during booking.

poojas.name: Vastu Shanti Pooja
poojas.description: Ritual to harmonize home energy and directions for prosperity.

faq.question: When should Naamkaran be performed?
faq.answer: Naamkaran is typically performed on the 11th, 12th, or 14th day after birth. The exact date is determined based on the baby's birth chart and family traditions.

faq.question: What do we need to prepare?
faq.answer: You need to prepare a clean, sacred space for the ceremony. Our pandit will bring all necessary items including puja materials, flowers, and sacred texts.

faq.question: Can we choose the name?
faq.answer: Yes, you can suggest names, and the pandit will help select the most auspicious one based on the child's birth chart and astrological considerations.

poojas.name: Satyanarayana Pooja
poojas.description: Monthly prosperity and peace ritual dedicated to Lord Vishnu.

faq.question: When should Naamkaran be performed?
faq.answer: Naamkaran is typically performed on the 11th, 12th, or 14th day after birth. The exact date is determined based on the baby's birth chart and family traditions.

faq.question: What do we need to prepare?
faq.answer: You need to prepare a clean, sacred space for the ceremony. Our pandit will bring all necessary items including puja materials, flowers, and sacred texts.

faq.question: Can we choose the name?
faq.answer: Yes, you can suggest names, and the pandit will help select the most auspicious one based on the child's birth chart and astrological considerations.

poojas.name: New Home Pooja
poojas.description: House warming ceremony for new homes to purify and bless the dwelling.

faq.question: When should Naamkaran be performed?
faq.answer: Naamkaran is typically performed on the 11th, 12th, or 14th day after birth. The exact date is determined based on the baby's birth chart and family traditions.

faq.question: What do we need to prepare?
faq.answer: You need to prepare a clean, sacred space for the ceremony. Our pandit will bring all necessary items including puja materials, flowers, and sacred texts.

faq.question: Can we choose the name?
faq.answer: Yes, you can suggest names, and the pandit will help select the most auspicious one based on the child's birth chart and astrological considerations.

poojas.name: Amavasya Tarpanam
poojas.description: A sacred ancestral ritual performed on Amavasya (New Moon day) to offer gratitude and peace to departed ancestors (Pitru Devatas). This ritual helps remove Pitru Dosha and brings blessings for family well-being.

faq.question: Who should perform Amavasya Tarpanam?
faq.answer: Traditionally, male descendants perform the ritual, but women can also perform it depending on family customs and guidance from the pandit.

faq.question: Is it mandatory every month?
faq.answer: It is not mandatory, but performing Amavasya Tarpanam regularly is believed to bring ancestral blessings and reduce obstacles.

faq.question: Can this be done at home?
faq.answer: Yes, Amavasya Tarpanam can be performed at home or near a water body under the guidance of a pandit.

poojas.name: Mahalaya Tarpanam
poojas.description: Mahalaya Tarpanam is a highly significant ancestral ritual performed during Pitru Paksha, especially on Mahalaya Amavasya. It is believed that ancestors visit the earthly realm on this day, and offerings made bring them peace and blessings.

faq.question: Why is Mahalaya Tarpanam important?
faq.answer: It is considered the most powerful day to honor ancestors, as offerings made on this day are believed to reach all departed souls of the lineage.

faq.question: What happens if Mahalaya Tarpanam is missed?
faq.answer: If missed, it can be performed on another suitable day during Pitru Paksha with guidance from a pandit.

faq.question: Can this ritual remove Pitru Dosha?
faq.answer: Yes, Mahalaya Tarpanam is believed to significantly reduce Pitru Dosha and bring peace, health, and prosperity to the family.

poojas.name: Rudrabhishek Pooja
poojas.description: Sacred abhishek ritual performed on Shiva Linga with holy substances to invoke Lord Shiva's blessings for health, prosperity, and moksha.

faq.question: What is Rudrabhishek?
faq.answer: Rudrabhishek is a sacred ritual where the Shiva Linga is bathed with holy substances like milk, honey, ghee, and water while chanting Vedic mantras from the Rudra Ashtadhyayi.

faq.question: When is the best time to perform Rudrabhishek?
faq.answer: Mondays, Pradosham days, Mahashivaratri, and Shravan month are the most auspicious times. It can also be performed on one's birthday or during times of difficulty.

faq.question: What are the benefits?
faq.answer: Rudrabhishek removes sins, cures diseases, wards off evil, brings peace, and helps attain moksha. It is especially recommended for those with Shani dosha or health issues.

poojas.name: Lakshmi Kubera Homam
poojas.description: Powerful fire ritual invoking both Goddess Lakshmi and Lord Kubera for wealth, abundance, and financial prosperity.

faq.question: Who should perform this homam?
faq.answer: Anyone seeking financial growth, business prosperity, debt relief, or career advancement can benefit from this homam.

faq.question: Can this be done for a new business?
faq.answer: Yes, it is highly recommended before launching a new business, taking a loan, or making significant investments.

faq.question: What is the best day to perform this?
faq.answer: Fridays, Purnima, and Diwali are especially auspicious for Lakshmi Kubera Homam. Our pandits can also identify a suitable muhurta based on your horoscope.

poojas.name: Naamkaran Sanskar
poojas.description: Sacred naming ceremony for newborns, one of the 16 Shodasha Samskaras, performed to bless the child with a meaningful, auspicious name.

faq.question: When should Naamkaran be performed?
faq.answer: Naamkaran is typically performed on the 11th, 12th, or 14th day after birth. The exact date is determined based on the baby's birth chart and family traditions.

faq.question: What do we need to prepare?
faq.answer: You need to prepare a clean, sacred space for the ceremony. Our pandit will bring all necessary puja materials, flowers, and sacred texts.

faq.question: Can we choose the name?
faq.answer: Yes, you can suggest names and the pandit will help select the most auspicious one based on the child's birth chart and astrological considerations.

poojas.name: Vivah Panchami Pooja
poojas.description: Auspicious pooja celebrating the divine wedding of Lord Rama and Goddess Sita, performed to seek blessings for marital harmony, love, and prosperity.

faq.question: Who should perform this pooja?
faq.answer: Couples seeking marital bliss, individuals looking to remove obstacles in marriage, and devotees of Lord Rama can all benefit from this pooja.

faq.question: When is it performed?
faq.answer: It is traditionally performed on Vivah Panchami (5th day of Shukla Paksha in Margashirsha month), though it can be arranged on other auspicious days too.

faq.question: Can unmarried individuals perform this?
faq.answer: Yes, unmarried individuals can perform this pooja to seek blessings for finding a suitable life partner and to remove delays in marriage.

poojas.name: Sudarshana Homam
poojas.description: Powerful Vedic fire ritual invoking Lord Vishnu's divine Sudarshana Chakra to destroy negative energies, evil eye, and black magic.

faq.question: When should Sudarshana Homam be performed?
faq.answer: It is recommended when experiencing persistent illness, financial losses, family conflicts, bad dreams, or suspected evil eye and negative energies.

faq.question: How does this homam protect us?
faq.answer: The Sudarshana Chakra is Lord Vishnu's weapon of divine protection. The homam invokes this energy to create a protective shield around the family and home.

faq.question: Can it be combined with other poojas?
faq.answer: Yes, Sudarshana Homam is often performed alongside Mrityunjaya Homam or Navagraha Homam for comprehensive protection and healing.

poojas.name: Annaprashana Sanskar
poojas.description: The sacred first rice-feeding ceremony for infants, one of the Shodasha Samskaras, marking the transition from milk to solid food with divine blessings.

included: Blessings for child's health and growth

faq.question: At what age is Annaprashana performed?
faq.answer: Traditionally performed in the 6th month for boys and the 5th or 7th month for girls. The exact timing can vary based on family customs and the child's health.

faq.question: What food is offered during the ceremony?
faq.answer: Rice kheer (payasam) mixed with ghee is typically offered as the first solid food. Our pandit will guide you on the preparation and offering procedure.

faq.question: Is a muhurta required?
faq.answer: Yes, an auspicious muhurta is selected based on the child's birth chart. Please share the birth details during booking so our pandit can calculate the best time.

poojas.name: Mrityunjaya Homam
poojas.description: Powerful Vedic fire ritual chanting the Mahamrityunjaya mantra to invoke Lord Shiva's blessings for protection from illness, accidents, and untimely death.

faq.question: Who should perform Mrityunjaya Homam?
faq.answer: It is recommended for people recovering from serious illness, those with health fears, individuals with Shani or Rahu dosha, or anyone seeking divine protection for their family.

faq.question: What is the significance of the Mahamrityunjaya mantra?
faq.answer: The Mahamrityunjaya mantra is one of the most powerful Vedic mantras addressed to Lord Shiva in his aspect as the conqueror of death. It promotes healing, longevity, and liberation.

faq.question: Can it be performed for someone who is ill?
faq.answer: Yes, it can be performed on behalf of a sick family member. The yajaman (performer) can dedicate the homam for the health and recovery of their loved one.

poojas.name: Griha Pravesh Ceremony
poojas.description: Auspicious house-entering ceremony combining Vastu Shanti, Ganapathi Pooja, and fire rituals to purify and consecrate a new home before moving in.

faq.question: When is the best muhurta for Griha Pravesh?
faq.answer: Auspicious months include Chaitra, Vaisakha, Jyeshtha, Magha, and Phalguna. The exact muhurta is calculated based on the owner's birth chart and the direction of the main entrance.

faq.question: What should we prepare before the ceremony?
faq.answer: Ensure the house is clean and a dedicated pooja space is set up. You may also prepare a new clay pot, mango leaves, and coconut for the threshold ritual. Our pandits will guide you on everything else.

faq.question: Is Griha Pravesh different from Vastu Shanti?
faq.answer: Vastu Shanti is a component within Griha Pravesh. The full Griha Pravesh ceremony is more comprehensive, including fire rituals, deity invocations, and the actual house-entering ritual.

poojas.name: Chandi Homam
poojas.description: Powerful fire ritual invoking Goddess Chandi (Durga) to destroy negative forces and bring victory, courage, and protection.

faq.question: When to perform?
faq.answer: Navratri, full moon, or Tuesdays are auspicious for Chandi Homam.

poojas.name: Ayush Homam
poojas.description: Vedic fire ritual for longevity, health, and vitality. Invokes divine blessings for a long and disease-free life.

faq.question: Best time?
faq.answer: Birthdays, after recovery from illness, or during Dhanurmasam are ideal.

poojas.name: Saraswati Homam
poojas.description: Sacred fire ritual to invoke Goddess Saraswati for wisdom, knowledge, education success, and creative arts.

faq.question: When for exams?
faq.answer: Perform before exams, on Vasant Panchami, or Wednesdays for best results.

poojas.name: Diwali Lakshmi Pooja
poojas.description: Auspicious Lakshmi Pooja on Diwali night to welcome Goddess of wealth and prosperity into your home.

faq.question: Best muhurta?
faq.answer: Lakshmi Pooja is performed on Diwali evening during Pradosh Kaal.

poojas.name: Ganesh Chaturthi Pooja
poojas.description: Installation and worship of Lord Ganesha idol during the 10-day festival. Includes Sthapana, daily puja, and Visarjan.

faq.question: 10-day or 1-day?
faq.answer: We offer both full 10-day puja and single-day installation packages.

poojas.name: Navratri Durga Pooja
poojas.description: Nine nights of Devi worship. Daily puja, Garba guidance, and Kanya Pooja. Celebrates the triumph of good over evil.

faq.question: Which Navratri?
faq.answer: Both Chaitra (spring) and Sharad (autumn) Navratri are equally auspicious.

poojas.name: Mundan Sanskar
poojas.description: Sacred head-tonsure ceremony (Chudakarana), one of the 16 samskaras. Performed for spiritual purity and ancestral blessings.

faq.question: When to perform?
faq.answer: Typically in 1st, 3rd, or 5th year. Odd years and odd months are preferred.

poojas.name: Upanayana Sanskar
poojas.description: Sacred thread ceremony (Janeu). Marks the beginning of Vedic education and spiritual initiation for the child.

faq.question: Age for Upanayana?
faq.answer: Typically performed between 7-16 years depending on community and region.

poojas.name: Vehicle Pooja
poojas.description: Blessing ceremony for new cars, bikes, or any vehicle. Invokes divine protection for safe travels.

faq.question: At home or showroom?
faq.answer: Can be performed at your home, showroom, or any preferred location.

poojas.name: Business Opening Pooja
poojas.description: Auspicious ceremony for new shop, office, or business venture. Ganapathi and Lakshmi worship for prosperity.

faq.question: Best day?
faq.answer: Wednesdays (Ganesha), Thursdays (Lakshmi), or Muhurta days are ideal.

poojas.name: Engagement / Tilak Ceremony
poojas.description: Sacred engagement ceremony marking the formal commitment between families. Includes Tilak, exchange of gifts, and blessings.

faq.question: Customs vary?
faq.answer: Yes, we adapt to your community traditions - North Indian, South Indian, or regional customs.

poojas.name: Graha Shanti (Marriage)
poojas.description: Planetary peace ritual before marriage to remove doshas and ensure harmony. Often done as part of wedding preparations.

faq.question: When to perform?
faq.answer: Typically 1-2 days before the wedding or during the wedding ceremonies.

poojas.name: Purnima Tarpanam
poojas.description: Ancestral offering ritual on full moon day. Honors departed souls and seeks their blessings for family prosperity.

faq.question: Monthly?
faq.answer: Yes, performed on every Purnima (full moon) for ancestral blessings.

poojas.name: Krishna Janmashtami Pooja
poojas.description: Birth celebration of Lord Krishna. Midnight puja, Jhulan, and Dahi Handi arrangements with traditional fervor.

faq.question: Midnight puja?
faq.answer: Yes, we perform the birth celebration puja at midnight as per tradition.

poojas.name: Rama Navami Pooja
poojas.description: Birth celebration of Lord Rama. Rama Katha, Sita Rama Kalyanam, and devotional recitations.

faq.question: Best time?
faq.answer: Rama Navami falls in Chaitra month. Midday puja is considered most auspicious.

poojas.name: Hanuman Chalisa Path
poojas.description: Recitation of the 40-verse Hanuman Chalisa for strength, protection, and removal of obstacles. Can include Sunderkand.

faq.question: Sunderkand too?
faq.answer: Yes, we offer Hanuman Chalisa, Sunderkand, or both as per your preference.

poojas.name: Sankranti / Pongal Pooja
poojas.description: Harvest festival worship. Surya Pooja, Bhogi, Thai Pongal, and thanksgiving rituals for prosperity.

faq.question: Regional variations?
faq.answer: We tailor the ceremony to Tamil Pongal, Telugu Sankranti, or North Indian customs.

poojas.name: Seemantham / Baby Shower
poojas.description: Traditional baby shower ceremony (5th, 7th, or 9th month of pregnancy). Blessings for mother and child.

faq.question: When to perform?
faq.answer: 5th, 7th, or 9th month of pregnancy - odd months are preferred.

poojas.name: Dhanteras Pooja
poojas.description: Worship of Lord Dhanvantari and Goddess Lakshmi on Dhanteras. Auspicious for buying gold and new items.

faq.question: Best muhurta?
faq.answer: Dhanteras evening during Pradosh is ideal for Lakshmi Pooja.

poojas.name: Dhanvantari Homam
poojas.description: Fire ritual dedicated to Lord Dhanvantari, the divine physician. For health, healing, and hospital success.

faq.question: For hospitals?
faq.answer: Yes, often performed for new clinics, hospitals, or before major surgeries.

poojas.name: Surya Homam
poojas.description: Fire ritual to invoke Surya (Sun God) for vitality, success, government favor, and removal of Surya dosha.

faq.question: Best day?
faq.answer: Sundays, especially during Uttarayan, are most auspicious for Surya Homam.

poojas.name: Vidyarambha Sanskar
poojas.description: Sacred initiation into learning. Child's first formal introduction to letters and education (Aksharabhyasam).

faq.question: Age for Vidyarambha?
faq.answer: Usually performed when the child is 3-5 years, often on Vijaya Dashami.

poojas.name: Karva Chauth Pooja
poojas.description: Fasting and moonrise puja by married women for husband's longevity. Katha, puja, and moon sighting ritual.

faq.question: At home?
faq.answer: Yes, we come to your home for the evening Karva Chauth puja and moon sighting.

poojas.name: Durga Homam
poojas.description: Powerful fire ritual invoking Goddess Durga for protection, victory over obstacles, and empowerment.

faq.question: Best time?
faq.answer: Navratri, Tuesdays, or Fridays are ideal for Durga Homam.

poojas.name: Onam Pooja
poojas.description: Kerala harvest festival. Onam Thrikkakkara, Athachamayam, Pookalam, and traditional Onam sadya blessings.

faq.question: Kerala traditions?
faq.answer: Yes, we follow traditional Kerala Onam rituals and Malayalam mantras.

poojas.name: Lakshmi Abhishek
poojas.description: Sacred bathing of Goddess Lakshmi idol with panchamrit and flowers for wealth and prosperity.

faq.question: Best day?
faq.answer: Fridays and full moon days are most auspicious for Lakshmi Abhishek.
